ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for a Subway Veggie Delite Soup Meal combo. FOODON_00002473 (vegetable soup food product) is the closest defensible parent for the soup component. The sandwich component is not separately coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Vegetable soup) is used as the closest parent for the soup component of this meal set. No composite meal-set code exists in FoodEx2 for this specific Subway product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Vegetable soup) is the closest available concept. The full meal set (soup + sandwich) is not represented as a single SNOMED CT concept.

preparation_method: served with bread roll or sandwich
main_ingredients: mixed vegetables (lettuce, tomato, cucumber, green pepper, onion, olive)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Veggie Delite Soup Meal serving consists of approximately 450 g total, comprising a bowl of vegetable soup (approximately 250 g) paired with a Veggie Delite sandwich or roll (approximately 200 g).

nutrient_source.title: Subway Veggie Delite 6-inch sandwich and vegetable soup — USDA FoodData Central composite estimation; Subway Singapore published nutrition data used as secondary reference
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy); Subway Restaurants nutritional information
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for the Subway Veggie Delite Soup Meal as a composite. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA SR Legacy entry for vegetable soup (USDA NDB 06116, Soup, vegetable beef, canned, condensed — adapted to vegetable-only broth) and (2) USDA FoodData Central entry for Subway Veggie Delite 6-inch sandwich components (lettuce, tomato, cucumber, green pepper, onion, bread). Per-100g values represent a weighted composite of the full meal set at the stated 450 g serving size. Values are moderate-confidence estimates. Subway Singapore nutritional disclosures were used to cross-check sodium and energy figures.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — vegetable soup and mixed-grain bread analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Veggie Delite Soup Meal. GI of 45 is estimated as a weighted composite: vegetable soup component assigned GI ~40 (low; per University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for vegetable soup) and the bread/sandwich component assigned GI ~50 (low-medium; per University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed-grain sandwich bread). Weighted average across the 450 g serving (soup ~56% by weight, bread ~44%) yields composite GI ~45, classified as low. Glycemic load calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 54.0) / 100 = 24.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; full meal serving delivers approximately 1395 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a single Veggie Delite Soup Meal serving may contribute approximately 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; portion control or requesting reduced-sodium soup preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) and moderate dietary fibre (1.8 g per 100 g) support glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: The Veggie Delite Soup Meal has a low GI of approximately 45 and provides dietary fibre from vegetables and bread, making it a relatively suitable meal option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in standard portions; however, the glycemic load per full serving (~24) is moderate and should be factored into daily carbohydrate budgeting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.3 g per 100 g), no trans fat, and high vegetable content support cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The Veggie Delite Soup Meal is low in saturated fat and contains no trans fat, with a predominantly vegetable-based composition that is consistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns; the main cardiovascular concern is the moderate sodium load from the soup broth, which should be monitored in individuals with existing cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Veggie Delite Soup Meal is a vegetarian fast-food meal set offered by Subway restaurants in Singapore, consisting of a Veggie Delite sandwich or bread roll paired with a bowl of vegetable soup. The Veggie Delite sandwich features a selection of fresh vegetables — typically lettuce, tomato, cucumber, green pepper, red onion, and olives — served on a choice of Subway bread. The accompanying vegetable soup is a warm, lightly seasoned broth-based soup containing mixed vegetables. The meal is assembled to order and is not cooked at high heat beyond the soup preparation. The texture profile combines the soft chewiness of the bread with the crisp freshness of raw vegetables and the warm, smooth consistency of the soup. Nutritionally, the meal is low in fat and saturated fat, moderate in carbohydrates, and provides dietary fibre and micronutrients including vitamin A, vitamin C, and iron from the vegetable components. The sodium content is notable due to the soup broth and should be considered by individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index of the meal is estimated at approximately 45 (low GI), making it a relatively suitable option for individuals managing blood glucose levels. The meal is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal-certified at Subway Singapore outlets. It is not suitable for individuals with gluten intolerance due to the bread component. This meal is commonly consumed as a light lunch or dinner option and is positioned as a healthier fast-food alternative in the Singapore market.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source entry exists for the Subway Veggie Delite Soup Meal as a composite product. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for vegetable soup and Subway Veggie Delite sandwich ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and cross-checked against Subway Singapore nutritional disclosures, but exact per-100g values may vary by outlet, bread choice, and soup formulation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for vegetable soup and Subway Veggie Delite sandwich components. Exact values may vary by bread choice, sauce additions, and soup batch. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero by product formulation for vegetable-based Subway products). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero as this is a plant-based meal with no animal-derived ingredients in the core components). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero as this is a plant-based meal; no animal-derived B12 sources present). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero; no fortified dairy or animal sources present in this vegetarian meal). 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and are moderate-confidence values. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific Subway meal set was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. 5. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical Subway 6-inch sandwich (~200 g) plus a standard soup serving (~250 g). 6. The food is listed under two field survey data restaurants: Subway - Funan and Funan - Mix & Match. The Mix & Match listing may represent a customised or repackaged version of the same product. 7. Sodium per serving (~1395 mg) is a significant clinical consideration for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient profile from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy vegetable soup and Subway Veggie Delite sandwich component data.
Estimated GI of 45 using University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for vegetable soup (~40) and mixed-grain sandwich bread (~50), weighted by serving proportion.
Added Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names to satisfy Singaporean dish multilingual requirement.
Populated aliases_by_language with Tamil and Hindi entries and verified all appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Documented zero-value justifications for trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg in notes.
Set all ontology match_type values to allowed values only (closest_parent) with gap notes.
Ensured glycemic_index is numeric and greater than zero (45) with gi_category 'low'.
Documented all -1 sentinel values are not present; all micronutrients estimated from USDA component data and given numeric values.
